author: @gusty9053
text: A small reminder: Bioware was at the time the studio famous for Dragon Age Origins (the good one) and Mass Effect, massive RPGs and EA decided they were the "perfect studio" to develop an online only looter shooter or whatever mess Anthem ended up being.

replies.author: @maxpudlowski8820
replies.text: That bioware got replaced by the dei hires.

replies.author: @gusty9053
replies.text:
@maxpudlowski8820 Oh i am well aware, it's just that in this instance and at that time EA was just as much to blame as Bioware for Anthem coming out a complete mess. EA deciding this game should be made by a studio that was known for making, arguably, the opposite style of game, was typical EA stupidity. 
The fact that Bioware went down the "DIE" route straight into the dumpster, at maximum speed, is entirely on the Bioware leadership and i won't waste a second mourning "current day" "Bioware" going under.

replies.author: @Doomspud-IIC
replies.text:
That was the main thing that ruined Anthem. It was basically half of two different games haphazardly glued together. And they clearly didn't fit at all. Because one half is a typical Bioware story focused RPG, and the other is an MMO style looter shooter. So the multiplayer parts got in the way of the story elements, and the story parts got in the way of the multiplayer elements. Anyone that wanted to enjoy the story and lore were stuck grinding for pointless loot in between. And anyone that just wanted to go shoot and loot with their friends had to constantly sit through pointless cutscenes in between.

You can even see the foundation of a companion system like Bioware games usually have. There are several other Lancers you meet in town, that totally seem like they're supposed be involved in the story, and join you on missions. But they never do, because you can only ever have other players in your squad. So instead of getting to know cool characters like Garrus or Morrigan, you're stuck teaming up with with xXxRescistSlur42069xXx and his constant mic breathing.

If Bioware had been able to just pick the half they were actually good at, and make it the whole game, it could have actually been really good. Because there was actually a lot of potential hidden in that pile of junk. But, both Bioware and EA were too blinded by the absurd profits Destiny 2 was pulling in at the time, so they just made a mess instead.

author: @Aetrion
text:
I installed it today just to see if I had rose tinted glasses about it, because I remember the actual gameplay of Anthem being pretty damn fun. It actually was fun. I've played a lot of these third person shooter RPG hybrids, and just the movement, shooting, abilities etc. in Anthem all work really well and are great.
The problem with the game was that the setting was forgettable, the progression, loot and build making sucked, and there was just a general lack of content in the game. The game has really solid bones and it's kind of sad that they did nothing with it.

author: @thrrax
text:
I remember that back when I was working in EA, the local branch of the Anthem QA was right next to us, and they were like 5 people in total, team lead included. They always worked 17-02 since they needed to work at the same time as the devs from abroad, mainly the main Bioware HQ, to be in sync. It was some sort of weird white box testing (for those who don't know, white box is when you have both devs and QA working on the same row, basically seated next to each other in an alternating fashion), since apparently the abroad team didn't have a dedicated QA.

 I remember them being uber secretive, even though we could see the whole time what they were doing and shit, and the game looking and working like shit. 

I also sadly remember that I got to have a sneak peak of the early Mass Effect Andromeda builds, and then seeing what they ended up making... I almost cried. The days of BG, DA and ME's Bioware were long gone.

author: @DreadPirateMorgan
text: I still say the rot goes all the way back to ME:2, which was like a lobotomized version of ME:1 with almost no genuine RPG leveling or gear customization.  It also took favorite characters from the first game and turned them into literal furniture that sat around listening while the new characters came up with the dumbest quests ever.
